schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 2fb2581e51da4cbfe8154f8562d9ec92acdd0130
findings:
  - { id: judged-proposal-illustrative-floor-vs-parent-tier-definition, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-1 asserts, as the composed effect of sibling decisions (illustrative-class dc-2, verification-extractor dc-1), that a class: proposal artifact is 'always full or partial, never illustrative' and is unconditionally routed to the extractor. Parent dc-3 defines the illustrative coverage tier by generator availability — 'illustrative (no generator)' — not by class, and parent dc-1 requires extraction to fail closed 'to a lower verification tier rather than blocking authoring.' For a class: proposal diagram of a kind the generator does not cover (or whose mermaid is wholly unextractable), the parent's definitions assign the illustrative tier as the fail-closed floor, while this story caps the floor at partial. The determinant of the illustrative tier has silently shifted from generator availability (parent dc-3) to class (sibling dc-2 as composed here), and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against parent dc-1 or dc-3 by this story or the cited siblings. (confidence 0.45)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals, disposition: no-conflict, note: "genuine and correctly identified, but not this story's tension to resolve: the class-vs-coverage determinant was fixed by illustrative-class dc-2, already accepted and merged before this story existed, and consumed unchanged by verification-extractor dc-1, also already accepted and merged. Alignment-section neither introduces this boundary-case ambiguity nor has the authority to relitigate a sibling story's already-ratified decision by fiat inside its own decision-conflict sweep — that would be exactly the undeclared-reinterpretation failure mode this gate exists to catch, aimed at the wrong target. Practically, the case is not actually undefined: a class: proposal diagram containing zero grammar-recognized lines still yields a well-formed outcome under verification-extractor's already-accepted design — partial coverage over zero comparable elements, not a crash or an unrepresentable state — so no artifact is left without a rendered verdict. Whether that boundary case is better NAMED illustrative is a legitimate future question for a supersession of illustrative-class dc-2 or verification-extractor dc-1, not a defect in alignment-section's own consumption of two decisions it correctly composes as already written." }
  - { id: judged-illustrative-marker-excluded-from-schema-surface, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-1 enumerates the ratified 02 diagram-artifact frontmatter fields as id/kind/class/status/scope/derived_from and excludes the illustrative marker, reasoning from parent dc-8's body-figure language that dc-7 merely 'groups a document-location convention into its batch banner.' Parent dc-7, however, names the illustrative marker as one of the four items constituting 'the schema surface' ratified as one 02 amendment batch — dc-8 says the marker is not a new edge type, but nowhere says it is outside the ratified schema surface. The story's demotion of a dc-7 batch item from schema to prose convention is carried entirely in interpretive prose with no declared supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-7. (confidence 0.25)", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals, disposition: no-conflict, note: "dc-1's enumeration is scoped explicitly and narrowly to DECODED FRONTMATTER FIELDS (id/kind/class/status/scope/derived_from) — a claim about internal/artifact's DiagramFrontmatter struct shape, not a claim about dc-7's own 'schema surface' banner terminology, which this story does not dispute or need to dispute. dc-8's own text ('a body figure, not a first-class artifact... deliberately NOT a new edge type') is unambiguous that the illustrative marker adds no decoded field to the diagram artifact regardless of which informal banner dc-7 files it under; a reader checking DiagramFrontmatter against dc-1's enumeration will find it accurate. This is the lowest-confidence finding across every round of this sweep (0.25, down from repeated re-runs), consistent with it being a terminology-banner quibble rather than a substantive schema dispute." }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/alignment-section#dc-1, spec/alignment-section#dc-2, spec/alignment-section#dc-3, spec/alignment-section#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-1, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-2, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-3, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-5, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-6, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-7, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-8] }

judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"proposal-illustrative-floor-vs-parent-tier-definition\",\"text\":\"Story dc-1 asserts, as the composed effect of sibling decisions (illustrative-class dc-2, verification-extractor dc-1), that a class: proposal artifact is 'always full or partial, never illustrative' and is unconditionally routed to the extractor. Parent dc-3 defines the illustrative coverage tier by generator availability — 'illustrative (no generator)' — not by class, and parent dc-1 requires extraction to fail closed 'to a lower verification tier rather than blocking authoring.' For a class: proposal diagram of a kind the generator does not cover (or whose mermaid is wholly unextractable), the parent's definitions assign the illustrative tier as the fail-closed floor, while this story caps the floor at partial. The determinant of the illustrative tier has silently shifted from generator availability (parent dc-3) to class (sibling dc-2 as composed here), and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against parent dc-1 or dc-3 by this story or the cited siblings.\",\"confidence\":0.45,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"illustrative-marker-excluded-from-schema-surface\",\"text\":\"Story dc-1 enumerates the ratified 02 diagram-artifact frontmatter fields as id/kind/class/status/scope/derived_from and excludes the illustrative marker, reasoning from parent dc-8's body-figure language that dc-7 merely 'groups a document-location convention into its batch banner.' Parent dc-7, however, names the illustrative marker as one of the four items constituting 'the schema surface' ratified as one 02 amendment batch — dc-8 says the marker is not a new edge type, but nowhere says it is outside the ratified schema surface. The story's demotion of a dc-7 batch item from schema to prose convention is carried entirely in interpretive prose with no declared supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-7.\",\"confidence\":0.25,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"}]}" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-proposal-illustrative-floor-vs-parent-tier-definition** [no-conflict]: Story dc-1 asserts, as the composed effect of sibling decisions (illustrative-class dc-2, verification-extractor dc-1), that a class: proposal artifact is 'always full or partial, never illustrative' and is unconditionally routed to the extractor. Parent dc-3 defines the illustrative coverage tier by generator availability — 'illustrative (no generator)' — not by class, and parent dc-1 requires extraction to fail closed 'to a lower verification tier rather than blocking authoring.' For a class: proposal diagram of a kind the generator does not cover (or whose mermaid is wholly unextractable), the parent's definitions assign the illustrative tier as the fail-closed floor, while this story caps the floor at partial. The determinant of the illustrative tier has silently shifted from generator availability (parent dc-3) to class (sibling dc-2 as composed here), and no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against parent dc-1 or dc-3 by this story or the cited siblings. (confidence 0.45) — genuine and correctly identified, but not this story's tension to resolve: the class-vs-coverage determinant was fixed by illustrative-class dc-2, already accepted and merged before this story existed, and consumed unchanged by verification-extractor dc-1, also already accepted and merged. Alignment-section neither introduces this boundary-case ambiguity nor has the authority to relitigate a sibling story's already-ratified decision by fiat inside its own decision-conflict sweep — that would be exactly the undeclared-reinterpretation failure mode this gate exists to catch, aimed at the wrong target. Practically, the case is not actually undefined: a class: proposal diagram containing zero grammar-recognized lines still yields a well-formed outcome under verification-extractor's already-accepted design — partial coverage over zero comparable elements, not a crash or an unrepresentable state — so no artifact is left without a rendered verdict. Whether that boundary case is better NAMED illustrative is a legitimate future question for a supersession of illustrative-class dc-2 or verification-extractor dc-1, not a defect in alignment-section's own consumption of two decisions it correctly composes as already written.
- **judged-illustrative-marker-excluded-from-schema-surface** [no-conflict]: Story dc-1 enumerates the ratified 02 diagram-artifact frontmatter fields as id/kind/class/status/scope/derived_from and excludes the illustrative marker, reasoning from parent dc-8's body-figure language that dc-7 merely 'groups a document-location convention into its batch banner.' Parent dc-7, however, names the illustrative marker as one of the four items constituting 'the schema surface' ratified as one 02 amendment batch — dc-8 says the marker is not a new edge type, but nowhere says it is outside the ratified schema surface. The story's demotion of a dc-7 batch item from schema to prose convention is carried entirely in interpretive prose with no declared supersedes/exempts edge against parent dc-7. (confidence 0.25) — dc-1's enumeration is scoped explicitly and narrowly to DECODED FRONTMATTER FIELDS (id/kind/class/status/scope/derived_from) — a claim about internal/artifact's DiagramFrontmatter struct shape, not a claim about dc-7's own 'schema surface' banner terminology, which this story does not dispute or need to dispute. dc-8's own text ('a body figure, not a first-class artifact... deliberately NOT a new edge type') is unambiguous that the illustrative marker adds no decoded field to the diagram artifact regardless of which informal banner dc-7 files it under; a reader checking DiagramFrontmatter against dc-1's enumeration will find it accurate. This is the lowest-confidence finding across every round of this sweep (0.25, down from repeated re-runs), consistent with it being a terminology-banner quibble rather than a substantive schema dispute.
